title: ByteWolf Plugin Tool Policy v0.1
description: The registry's fail-closed decision about what a plugin may actually consume. A plugin never ships its own ToolPolicy: the registry derives it by intersecting the manifest's 'requests' with the capability allowlist, so an unrequested or disallowed capability is simply never granted. See docs/workstreams/plugin-sdk.md.

contract_version.description: Pins the meaning of every field below.

plugin_id.description: The plugin this policy applies to.

granted.description: The capabilities the plugin may consume. An empty array is a valid, deliberate outcome: it means the plugin was granted nothing, not that the decision is missing.

denied.description: Capabilities the plugin requested but was refused, each with a reason, for audit. A denial is recorded, never silent.

reason.description: Why the request was refused, e.g. 'not on allowlist' or 'version unavailable'.

limits.description: Default execution budget the runtime enforces for this plugin's capability calls. Declared here so the timeout/rate boundary is versioned and auditable next to the grant, not buried in runtime config.

timeout_ms.description: Wall-clock ceiling for one capability call before it is cancelled.

rate_per_min.description: Maximum calls per minute across this plugin's grants.
